House Guide · For Huddy

Welcome to Ripley's house

Hey Huddy — thanks so much for taking care of our girl. Everything you'll need is in this guide, and we're a phone call away if anything comes up.

⚡ Emergency · Call First

If something feels off, call us

01

Meet Ripley

Ripley
Husky Mix · ~6 yrs

She's a spaz but gentle — happy, vocal, loves to be around her people. Standard husky energy: high enthusiasm, sheds a lot, and will absolutely take an opportunity to bolt if a door's open. Be ready for both ends of that personality.

02

Daily Routine

Morning

Breakfast

She has a doggy door, so she lets herself out into the yard to potty — no need to manage that. For breakfast, give her 1 cup of food using the scoop inside the food container under the kitchen sink, plus fresh water.

Anytime

Walk + yard play

Try to get her a real walk once a day — 30+ min is ideal. Use her leash from the back entry. She's a husky, so she'll pull. Important: she's a husky and overheats easily, but she'll want to keep going no matter how hot she gets — she won't quit on her own, so you have to call it. Walk early morning or after sunset, bring water, and head back if she's panting hard. Two mapped routes are below. The backyard is fenced too, so she can run around freely whenever — just keep an eye on the gate latch. Toys are around the house; she'll let you know what she wants.

Evening

Dinner

1 cup of food, same as breakfast. She'll use the doggy door on her own before settling in.

Night

Bedroom for the night

She sleeps in our bed/bedroom with us — you can let her do the same. She's crate-trained too if you'd rather have her sleep in the crate (it's set up in the main room), but she's used to having the run of the bedroom.

Here are the two routes we usually take her on — both start and end right at the front door. Tap a map to zoom or drag it around. Remember she's a husky: she gets hot fast but will keep pushing the pace anyway, so pick the shorter loop on warm days and don't let her talk you into "just a little farther."

The long loop

~2.6 mi
Bigger neighborhood loop — best saved for a cool early morning when she's got energy to burn.

The short loop

~1.6 mi
Quicker loop — the safer pick for warm afternoons or if she's already panting.
03

Around the House

Food

Under the kitchen sink

Black container with the clear front holds her food. The 1-cup scoop is right inside. Treats (Milk-Bone and freeze-dried Beef Liver) live in the same cabinet — feel free to use those for good behavior or just because.

Water

Water station

She has a water fountain plus a backup bowl. Keep the fountain topped up — she drinks a lot, especially after walks. The fountain is plugged in, just refill the reservoir as needed.

Back Entry

Leashes & trash bins

Her leash and harness hang on the hooks here. Trash bins live in this same spot — remember Sunday night is trash night. Watch the door when going in/out, this is her favorite escape opportunity.

Crate

Her crate setup

Set up in the main room with blankets — she's crate-trained and totally fine in it. When she goes in, give her the teeth-cleaning bone — she expects it and it keeps her settled. She can comfortably stay crated from about 9am to 4:30–5pm if you need her contained while you're out. One thing though — she's totally fine being crated for a work day, but she's not great if you come home after work and then put her right back in the crate after that. Plan to leave her out once you're back for the day. Otherwise she sleeps in our bedroom.

Supplies

First aid & extras

Pantry shelf has her flea/tick (Bravecto), heartworm (Sentinel), basic first aid supplies, extra treats, and her grooming bag. You shouldn't need any of it — she's not due for monthly meds during this stretch — but it's here just in case.

04

Husky Things to Know

She's vocal
Huskies talk. You'll hear howling, "rooing," and a full vocabulary of dramatic noises. Mostly when she's excited or wants something — not a sign anything's wrong.
Escape-prone
Always make sure the door is shut behind you and the yard gate is latched. If she sees an opening, she will take it. Keep her on leash anywhere outside the fenced yard.
Good traveler
She rides well in the car — no fuss. Her one habit: she'll try to climb up from the backseat and sit on the center console. Throw a towel or blanket over the console to protect it from her nails.
She sheds
A lot. Year-round. Sorry in advance.
05

Vet Info

(405) 525-8868

For after-hours emergencies, call us first — we can advise. If you can't reach us and it's urgent, the nearest 24-hour emergency vet is Neel Veterinary Hospital (2700 N MacArthur Blvd, OKC · open 24/7).

Trash

Plants

• The squash plant and the sunflowers

• Use water from the rain barrels — no need to use the hose
